mechanism_narrative: Elevated federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, mechanically compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show positive mean returns when FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.5% over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions and impending corporate earnings declines, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 20-day returns remain positive in 70% of instances where UNRATE increases by more than 0.2pp.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month growth in real retail sales and housing starts confirms broad-based consumer demand strength, supporting corporate revenue growth and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint positive signal yields negative median 60-day forward returns in 60% of historical occurrences.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the index of industrial production signals recovering manufacturing output and supply chain normalization, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases fail to predict positive 5-day forward returns with a Rank IC above 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrently elevated consumer price inflation and restrictive federal funds rates create a stagflationary environment that erodes real corporate margins and compresses equity multiples, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined high-inflation and high-rate regime produces positive median 20-day forward returns in 55% of observed periods.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects expanding economic activity and rising household income, which supports consumer spending and corporate earnings, yielding positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth shows no statistically significant positive correlation with 60-day forward returns (p-value > 0.10) over a 5-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: Two consecutive quarters of negative real GDP growth confirm a technical recession, triggering corporate earnings downgrades and risk-off portfolio rebalancing that produce negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 declines for two consecutive quarters
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 20-day returns are positive in 65% of quarters immediately following a confirmed two-quarter GDP decline.

mechanism_narrative: Synchronized month-over-month increases in industrial production and real retail sales indicate efficient supply-demand matching and inventory buildup, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The synchronized positive signal fails to predict positive 5-day forward returns with a hit rate above 55% over a 3-year rolling window.
